title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawdord, Snorkel
summary: Kobie Crawford argues that smaller models trained with reinforcement learning to use tools correctly and self-correct outperform much larger models that hallucinate. A 4B Snorkel-finetuned model trained with under $500 solved a SQL task that Qwen 3 235B failed, and single-table training transferred to multi-table tasks with significant benchmark gains.
writeup: Kobie Crawford of Snorkel makes the case that model behavior, not parameter count, drives task performance: smaller models trained with reinforcement learning to use tools correctly and self-correct can beat much larger models that hallucinate. The concrete result is a 4B Snorkel-finetuned model, trained for under $500, solving a SQL task that Qwen 3 235B failed. Training on single-table data transferred to multi-table tasks with significant benchmark gains, pointing to behavioral training as a cost-efficient alternative to scaling.
